ranking_criteria.locality: Prefer single-hunk, low-line-count fixes in core src/ files
ranking_criteria.semantic_clarity: Prefer clear bug semantics (off-by-one, missing guard, wrong field/container)
ranking_criteria.testability: Prefer fixes with nearby tests or paths exercised by existing proptest/unit suites
ranking_criteria.expressibility: Prefer candidates that map to marauders expression/statement mutations

ranked.rationale: One-token off-by-one range fix in writer core path; highly local and mutation-friendly.
Single-line wrong-container access causing panic; very clear semantics and easy expression-level mutation.
Canonical missing-empty-check leading to division by zero; compact statement-level mutation with clear runtime impact.
Missing seek-before-write in reader->writer transition; localized and realistically bug-inducing.
Clear base-case bug in empty-index search with dedicated regression test; straightforward statement mutation.
Low-memory None-handling panic fix in recursive insertion; good signal though slightly more context-coupled.
Wrong key field in upgrade metadata branching; expression-level and precise, but migration-path specific.
Off-by-one threshold in tree-count stopping condition; good candidate but embedded in larger tuning change.
Strong expression bug (`remove_smallest` vs `remove(item)`), but commit bundles multiple fixes in same function.
Concurrency/error-propagation short-circuit fix is valid but has weaker deterministic observability as mutation.
Real bug in upgrade rewrite logic with good provenance, but more structural and less direct to encode cleanly.
Search-window widening in tmp-node removal is plausible but semantically weaker and risks equivalent behavior.
